name: hayagriva-llm
description: Structured LLM metadata standard for Node.js packages — llm.package.json and llm.package.txt

cli.description: Starts the Hayagriva CLI, parsing command-line arguments and dispatching to the appropriate command handler. It initializes the application state, loads configuration from environment variables, and runs the selected operation such as generating LLM metadata or starting the observability dashboard.
cli.params: args?: string[] (optional command-line arguments, defaults to process.argv.slice(2))
cli.returns: Promise<void> (resolves when the CLI process completes)
cli.notes: Handles both synchronous and asynchronous operations, exits with appropriate status codes on errors

summary: Hayagriva-llm is a CLI tool that generates structured LLM metadata files (llm.package.json and llm.package.txt) for Node.js packages. It analyzes package manifests and source code to create comprehensive AI-readable descriptions of package functionality, helping AI systems better understand and interact with npm packages. The tool integrates seamlessly with existing package.json workflows and supports TypeScript analysis for accurate type information.
whenToUse: Use Hayagriva-llm when you want to make your Node.js package more discoverable and understandable by AI systems. It's particularly valuable for package authors who want their libraries to be properly interpreted by AI assistants, documentation generators, or automated code analysis tools. Choose this package when you need to generate standardized metadata that describes your package's functionality in a structured format that AI systems can parse and utilize.
reasonToUse: Improves AI discoverability of your package
Creates both JSON and text format descriptions
Supports TypeScript analysis for accurate type information
Provides CLI interface for easy integration into build processes
Offers both static and AI-powered analysis modes
Handles both CommonJS and ESM modules
useCases: Generating metadata for npm packages to improve AI assistant responses
Creating standardized package descriptions for automated documentation systems
Enhancing package searchability in AI-powered code repositories
Providing structured data for AI code completion tools
Documenting package functionality for machine learning model training
Automating metadata generation in CI/CD pipelines
Creating local observability dashboards for metadata analysis
Supporting TypeScript projects with accurate type information
Analyzing package functionality for automated testing tools
Improving IDE integration with AI-powered features

capabilities: Supports static TypeScript analysis via ts-morph
Offers AI-powered analysis using OpenRouter API
Provides CLI with multiple commands (generate, dashboard)
Handles both CommonJS and ESM modules
Reads package files from current working directory
Writes metadata files to package directory
configuration: Command-line options for mode, API key, model selection
Include source option for AI mode
limitations: AI mode requires OpenRouter API key
Static analysis may miss dynamic behavior
security: Stores OpenRouter API key in environment variables
No network requests except for AI analysis mode
Reads package files from current working directory
Writes metadata files to package directory
